extraction.one_line_summary: BOASSON HAGEN won from reduced sprint after LAMBRECHT's late breakaway acceleration with 7.5km left forced selection, eliminating pure sprinters.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step sent ALAPHILIPPE attacking at 20km on climb instead of protecting HODGE for sprint, revealing hierarchy shift.
LAMBRECHT's climbing accelerations in final 10km eliminated pure sprinters while preserving himself for breakaway, sophisticated neo-pro tactics.
Dimension Data positioned BOASSON HAGEN perfectly for reduced sprint, exploiting gap between climbers' breakaway and traditional sprint trains dissolving.
Bahrain-Merida paced hard in closing kilometers but lacked finisher in reduced group, wasted energy for rivals' benefit.

rider_signals.LAMBRECHT Bjorg: positive: At 21 years old initiated race-splitting accelerations with 20km left and stayed in winning break, climbs better than typical classics riders.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Quick-Step prioritized his GC moves over Hodge sprint setup at 20km, confirms protected status for stage race leadership.
rider_signals.HODGE Alvaro: negative: Team abandoned sprint preparation for him when race split, indicates secondary status within Quick-Step hierarchy.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Dropped when race accelerated on climb at 20km, signal of declining form or poor early-season condition.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Present in reduced group but not animating, appears in conservative recon mode for Ineos leadership decisions.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: negative: Initiated final sprint but faded badly, short punchy finish should suit him but lacks explosive power.
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: Won from reduced sprint after surviving climber's selection, retains explosive kick when fresher sprinters eliminated.
